title: toxicodendron celastroides
intro: Toxicodendron celastroides, also known as the poison sumac, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and China, characterized by its bright red berries and leaves that contain the toxic oil urushiol, which causes skin irritation and allergic reactions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for fever and pain. The health benefits of Toxicodendron celastroides include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce inflammation, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, but caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its toxic properties.
